steps.title: Identify the decisive contrast
steps.content: The passage contrasts two findings: on rocky planets, detectable NH₃ requires biological activity; on mini-Neptunes, it forms naturally from atmospheric conditions.

steps.title: Apply the distinction to the choices
steps.content: Since NH₃'s origin differs by planet type, the researchers would treat it as a biosignature on rocky planets (where it indicates life) but not on mini-Neptunes (where it occurs naturally)—the exact distinction in choice C. The other three ignore this distinction, reverse its implications, or contradict the passage's claim about natural formation. That matches choice C — C is correct.
